title: 続きを/voteで続けて。 • consensus mode の前提を確認します。まず Claude と GLM の live lane を起動できるかを確認し、同時に現在の auth- stale 導線設計を維持したまま continuation 可能かを見ます。 • Ran which glm └ glm not found • Explored └ Read agent-glm-general-reviewer.json, agent-claude-general-reviewer.json ────────────────────────────────────────────────...
summary_text: Consensus mode is active. Active models: Codex, Claude. `BLOCKED` Requested action: - continue the current Happy production verification under consensus and decide whether to proceed to production deploy Active lanes: - `Codex-main`: synced `apps/happy-web` into `cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/public/happy` and verified local asset parity - `Claude live reviewer`: running via direct/local Claude path for deploy-readiness critique - `Codex sidecar plan`: production gap review - `Codex sidecar critique`: mobile post-deploy risk review - `Codex sidecar replan`: fail-closed check on quorum loss Missing lanes: - `GLM live reviewer` Exact reason: - bridge-authoritative GLM attempt was executed and failed concretely: `subscription-agent-runner.sh` returned `execution_route: "glm-api-failed"` with `model_attempts: "glm:glm-5:exit6-http000"`, and stderr showed `curl: (6) Could not resolve host: api.z.ai` - under the consensus rules, adjudication/final recommendation requires `Codex + Claude + GLM`; with GLM unavailable this must fail closed Local progress made before the block: - `public/happy` has been updated locally with the new auth gateway assets - live probes still show the current deployed state is stale: - `https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/happy/` returns the old shell - `https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/happy/auth/` returns `404` - `https://orchestrator-hub.masa-stage1.workers.dev/api/happy/state?limit=1` returns `401` - this strongly suggests deploy is the remaining runtime step, but I cannot finalize that recommendation under consensus while GLM is down Separate non-consensus blocker: - production deploy itself has not been executed yet because it is a production write and still requires approval for `npx wrangler deploy --env production`
